threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look at the bus timetable instead of just checking your phone. Ain't nobody got time for that around here!
Caught a night bus home and a guy was arguing with a pigeon. 'I’m not afraid of you!' he yelled. The pigeon just stared. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a pack of kids using the U-Bahn ticket machines like it's an arcade game, pressing buttons like pros. This is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they have spontaneous techno parties in the middle of a park, complete with a makeshift DJ booth made from recycled pallets.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they hold their breath until the BART train shows up—never trusting the arrival times posted.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 this morning; a guy confidently recited Shakespeare to no one in particular while the bus lurched past a steam vent. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas where locals gather for afternoon vermut and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a new arrival by their confusion over the 4-way intersections — they stop at every corner, waiting for traffic lights that don't exist.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'bus stop' instead of a 'bus shelter'—can't help but grin when I hear that from newcomers.
Caught a group of teens on the 35 last night debating the best fried chicken in Brixton. Their loyalty to the greasy spot by the station was fierce.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in flip-flops and a winter parka arguing with a pigeon over a pretzel. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's common to see spontaneous dance-offs in the subway stations. Just another Monday with a side of creativity amid the chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're from here when they can name the exact BART station that starts the domino of delays—Civic Center is the classic, of course.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing a ukulele with a cat on his lap during a Muni ride. Cat didn’t care, but everyone else was captivated.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the park's always buzzing, but locals know the best time to visit is early morning when it's almost empty and magical.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by how they wait at the bus stop, checking their phone every minute instead of enjoying the street performances.
